title: S3 putObject with ACL: "public-read" — every uploaded object is world-readable

vulnerable.detail: putObject is called with ACL: "public-read", which makes the uploaded object readable by ANYONE on the internet who knows or guesses the URL. If user uploads (documents, avatars, exports) flow through here, they are silently exposed publicly. Uploads succeed and the app works, so it ships. Use ACL: "private" (the default) and serve objects through pre-signed URLs.

fixed.detail: The S3 put uses a non-public ACL (not "public-read") — the object is not world-readable.

title: cookie-session with httpOnly: false — the session cookie is readable from JavaScript (XSS session theft)

vulnerable.detail: cookie-session is configured with httpOnly: false, exposing the signed session cookie to JavaScript via document.cookie. Any XSS on the origin can read and exfiltrate the session and hijack the user's account. Set httpOnly: true (the default) for session cookies.

fixed.detail: cookie-session sets httpOnly: true — the session cookie is not exposed to document.cookie and cannot be read by client-side scripts.

title: cookie-session with secure: false — the session cookie is sent over plain HTTP and can be sniffed

vulnerable.detail: cookie-session is configured with secure: false, so the session cookie is sent over plain HTTP. On any non-TLS hop an attacker can read the signed session and hijack the user's authenticated session. Set secure: true in production (behind `app.set('trust proxy', 1)` if terminating TLS at a proxy).

fixed.detail: cookie-session sets secure: true — the signed session cookie is only ever transmitted over HTTPS.
